records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4289 - Imam (as) was asked about a man who swore an oath out of taqiyyah (dissimulation).
Imam (as) said: "If you fear for your life or property, then swear an oath to protect yourself with it. However, if you see that your oath will not protect you in any way, then do not swear to them."
records.arabicText: 4289 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ حَلَفَ تَقِيَّةً قَالَ "إِنْ خَشِيتَ عَلَى دَمِكَ وَ مَالِكَ فَاحْلِفْ تَرُدُّهُ عَنْكَ بِيَمِينِكَ فَإِنْ رَأَيْتَ أَنَّ يَمِينَكَ لاَ تَرُدُّ عَنْكَ شَيْئاً فَلاَ تَحْلِفْ لَهُمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4289 - Imam (as) was asked about a man who swore an oath out of taqiyyah (dissimulation).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If you fear for your life or property, then swear an oath to protect yourself with it. However, if you see that your oath will not protect you in any way, then do not swear to them."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4290 - Al-Halabi said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who makes a vow upon himself without specifying it.
Imam (as) said: "If you specify it, then it is as you specified. But if you do not specify anything, then it is of no consequence. However, if you said: 'It is upon me for the sake of Allah (swt),' then its expiation is the expiation of an oath."
records.arabicText: 4290 - وَ قَالَ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ : وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَجْعَلُ عَلَيْهِ نَذْراً وَ لاَ يُسَمِّيهِ قَالَ "إِنْ سَمَّيْتَهُ فَهُوَ مَا سَمَّيْتَ وَ إِنْ لَمْ تُسَمِّ شَيْئاً فَلَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ فَإِنْ قُلْتَ لِلَّهِ عَلَيَّ فَكَفَّارَةُ يَمِينٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4290 - Al-Halabi said: I asked Imam (as) about a man who makes a vow upon himself without specifying it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If you specify it, then it is as you specified. But if you do not specify anything, then it is of no consequence. However, if you said: 'It is upon me for the sake of Allah (swt),' then its expiation is the expiation of an oath."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4291 - Imam (as) said: "Any oath that is not intended for the sake of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, is of no consequence, whether it pertains to divorce or emancipation."
records.arabicText: 4291 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كُلُّ يَمِينٍ لاَ يُرَادُ بِهَا وَجْهُ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَلَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ، فِي طَلاَقٍ أَوْ عِتْقٍ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4291 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Any oath that is not intended for the sake of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, is of no consequence, whether it pertains to divorce or emancipation."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4292 - Imam (as) said regarding the expiation of an oath: "It is a mudd and a handful."
records.arabicText: 4292 - وَ قَالَ : "فِي كَفَّارَةِ اَلْيَمِينِ مُدٌّ وَ حَفْنَةٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4292 - Imam (as) said regarding the expiation of an oath:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is a mudd and a handful."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4293 - And regarding a man who swears an oath to the tax collector in order to protect his wealth.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 4293 - وَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْلِفُ لِصَاحِبِ اَلْعُشُورِ يُحْرِزُ بِذَلِكَ مَالَهُ قَالَ "نَعَمْ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4294 - I asked him about a woman who vowed to offer her wealth as an offering to the House of Allah (swt) if she lent some of her belongings to so-and-so. Then, some of her family members lent those belongings without her permission.
Imam (as) said: "She is not required to offer the offering. The true offering is what is made as a dedication to Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, as an offering to the Ka'bah. That is what must be fulfilled when it is made for Allah (swt). But matters like this are of no consequence, and an offering that does not mention the name of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, is not valid."
records.arabicText: 4294 - وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اِمْرَأَةٍ جَعَلَتْ مَالَهَا هَدْياً لِبَيْتِ اَللَّهِ إِنْ أَعَارَتْ مَتَاعاً لَهَا فُلاَنَةَ وَ فُلاَنَةَ فَأَعَارَ بَعْضُ أَهْلِهَا بِغَيْرِ أَمْرِهَا قَالَ "لَيْسَ عَلَيْهَا هَدْيٌ إِنَّمَا اَلْهَدْيُ مَا جُعِلَ لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ هَدْياً لِلْكَعْبَةِ فَذَلِكَ اَلَّذِي يُوفَى بِهِ إِذَا جُعِلَ لِلَّهِ وَ مَا كَانَ مِنْ أَشْبَاهِ هَذَا فَلَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ وَ لاَ هَدْيَ لاَ يُذْكَرُ فِيهِ اِسْمُ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4295 - Imam (as) was asked about a man who says, "I am obligated to offer a thousand camels" while he is in the state of ihram for a thousand Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "These are the steps of Satan."
And regarding a man who, while in ihram for Hajj, says, "I will offer this food as a gift,"
Imam (as) said: "It is of no consequence. Food is not offered as a gift."
Or if he says about a camel after it has been slaughtered, "This is an offering to the House of Allah (swt),"
Imam (as) said: "Offerings are made while the animals are alive, and they are not offered after they have become meat."
records.arabicText: 4295 - وَ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ عَلَيَّ أَلْفُ بَدَنَةٍ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ بِأَلْفِ حَجَّةٍ قَالَ "تِلْكَ خُطُوَاتُ اَلشَّيْطَانِ" وَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَقُولُ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ بِحَجَّةٍ أَوْ يَقُولُ أَنَا أُهْدِي هَذَا اَلطَّعَامَ قَالَ "لَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ إِنَّ اَلطَّعَامَ لاَ يُهْدَى أَوْ يَقُولُ لِجَزُورٍ بَعْدَ مَا نُحِرَتْ هُوَ هَدْيٌ لِبَيْتِ اَللَّهِ إِنَّمَا تُهْدَى اَلْبُدْنُ وَ هِيَ أَحْيَاءٌ وَ لَيْسَ تُهْدَى حِينَ صَارَتْ لَحْماً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4295 - Imam (as) was asked about a man who says,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I am obligated to offer a thousand camels" while he is in the state of ihram for a thousand Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "These are the steps of Satan."
And regarding a man who, while in ihram for Hajj, says, "I will offer this food as a gift,"
Imam (as) said: "It is of no consequence. Food is not offered as a gift."
Or if he says about a camel after it has been slaughtered, "This is an offering to the House of Allah (swt),"
Imam (as) said: "Offerings are made while the animals are alive, and they are not offered after they have become meat."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4296 - It is narrated in another tradition about a man who said: "No, by my father."
Imam (as) said: "He should seek forgiveness from Allah (swt)."
records.arabicText: 4296 - وَ رُوِيَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ لاَ وَ أَبِي قَالَ "يَسْتَغْفِرُ اَللَّهَ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4297 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"An oath is of two types. One type is when a man swears upon something that he is not obligated to do, yet he swears that he will do it.
The other type is when he swears upon something that he is obligated to do; in this case, if he does not fulfill it, he must offer expiation.
The second type has three categories. One of them is an oath for which a man will be rewarded if he swears falsely, another for which there is neither expiation nor reward, and the third for which there is no expiation but the punishment is entering the Fire.
As for the oath for which a man will be rewarded if he swears falsely and for which no expiation is required, it is when a person swears to protect the life of a Muslim or his property from an oppressor or a thief or anyone who intends harm.
As for the oath for which there is neither expiation nor reward, it is when a person swears upon something but later finds an option that is better than his oath, so he leaves the oath and chooses what is better.
And as for the oath whose punishment is entering the Fire, it is when a man swears falsely to take the wealth or rights of another Muslim unjustly. This is a 'Ghamus' oath, which necessitates the Fire, and there is no expiation for it in this world."
records.arabicText: 4297 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْيَمِينُ عَلَى وَجْهَيْنِ أَحَدُهُمَا أَنْ يَحْلِفَ اَلرَّجُلُ عَلَى شَيْءٍ لاَ يَلْزَمُهُ أَنْ يَفْعَلَ فَيَحْلِفُ أَنَّهُ يَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ اَلشَّيْءَ أَوْ يَحْلِفَ عَلَى مَا يَلْزَمُهُ أَنْ يَفْعَلَ فَعَلَيْهِ اَلْكَفَّارَةُ إِذَا لَمْ يَفْعَلْهُ وَ اَلْأُخْرَى عَلَى ثَلاَثَةِ أَوْجُهٍ فَمِنْهَا مَا يُؤْجَرُ اَلرَّجُلُ عَلَيْهِ إِذَا حَلَفَ كَاذِباً وَ مِنْهَا مَا لاَ كَفَّارَةَ عَلَيْهِ وَ لاَ أَجْرَ لَهُ وَ مِنْهَا مَا لاَ كَفَّارَةَ عَلَيْهِ فِيهَا وَ اَلْعُقُوبَةُ فِيهَا دُخُولُ اَلنَّارِ فَأَمَّا اَلَّتِي يُؤْجَرُ عَلَيْهَا اَلرَّجُلُ إِذَا حَلَفَ كَاذِباً وَ لاَ تَلْزَمُهُ اَلْكَفَّارَةُ فَهُوَ أَنْ يَحْلِفَ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي خَلاَصِ اِمْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ أَوْ خَلاَصِ مَالِهِ مِنْ مُتَعَدٍّ يَتَعَدَّى عَلَيْهِ مِنْ لِصٍّ أَوْ غَيْرِهِ وَ أَمَّا اَلَّتِي لاَ كَفَّارَةَ عَلَيْهِ فِيهَا وَ لاَ أَجْرَ لَهُ فَهُوَ أَنْ يَحْلِفَ اَلرَّجُلُ عَلَى شَيْءٍ ثُمَّ يَجِدُ مَا هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِنَ اَلْيَمِينِ فَيَتْرُكُ اَلْيَمِينَ وَ يَرْجِعُ إِلَى اَلَّذِي هُوَ خَيْرٌ وَ أَمَّا اَلَّتِي عُقُوبَتُهَا دُخُولُ اَلنَّارِ فَهُوَ أَنْ يَحْلِفَ اَلرَّجُلُ عَلَى مَالِ اِمْرِئٍ مُسْلِمٍ أَوْ عَلَى حَقِّهِ ظُلْماً فَهَذِهِ يَمِينٌ غَمُوسٌ تُوجِبُ اَلنَّارَ وَ لاَ كَفَّارَةَ عَلَيْهِ فِي اَلدُّنْيَا ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4297 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"An oath is of two types. One type is when a man swears upon something that he is not obligated to do, yet he swears that he will do it.
The other type is when he swears upon something that he is obligated to do; in this case, if he does not fulfill it, he must offer expiation.
The second type has three categories. One of them is an oath for which a man will be rewarded if he swears falsely, another for which there is neither expiation nor reward, and the third for which there is no expiation but the punishment is entering the Fire.
As for the oath for which a man will be rewarded if he swears falsely and for which no expiation is required, it is when a person swears to protect the life of a Muslim or his property from an oppressor or a thief or anyone who intends harm.
As for the oath for which there is neither expiation nor reward, it is when a person swears upon something but later finds an option that is better than his oath, so he leaves the oath and chooses what is better.
And as for the oath whose punishment is entering the Fire, it is when a man swears falsely to take the wealth or rights of another Muslim unjustly. This is a 'Ghamus' oath, which necessitates the Fire, and there is no expiation for it in this world."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4298 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said that a false oath leaves homes deserted of their inhabitants.
records.arabicText: 4298 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اَلْيَمِينُ اَلْكَاذِبَةُ تَدَعُ اَلدِّيَارَ بَلاَقِعَ مِنْ أَهْلِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4298 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said that
records.thaqalaynMatn: a false oath leaves homes deserted of their inhabitants.

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4299 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Whoever reveres Allah (swt) and refrains from swearing a false oath by Him (swt), Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, will grant him something better than what he has lost."
records.arabicText: 4299 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ أَجَلَّ اَللَّهَ أَنْ يَحْلِفَ بِهِ كَاذِباً أَعْطَاهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ خَيْراً مِمَّا ذَهَبَ مِنْهُ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4299 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever reveres Allah (swt) and refrains from swearing a false oath by Him (swt), Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, will grant him something better than what he has lost."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4300 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"No servant leaves something for the sake of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, and then finds himself in loss because of it."
records.arabicText: 4300 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "مَا تَرَكَ عَبْدٌ شَيْئاً لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَفَقَدَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4300 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "No servant leaves something for the sake of Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, and then finds himself in loss because of it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4301 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Whoever takes an oath in private should make an exception in private, and whoever takes an oath publicly should make an exception publicly."
records.arabicText: 4301 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ حَلَفَ سِرّاً فَلْيَسْتَثْنِ سِرّاً وَ مَنْ حَلَفَ عَلاَنِيَةً فَلْيَسْتَثْنِ عَلاَنِيَةً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4301 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever takes an oath in private should make an exception in private, and whoever takes an oath publicly should make an exception publicly."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4302 - Isma'il ibn Sa'd asked Abu al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) about a man who swears an oath, but his intention in the oath differs from the apparent wording.
Imam (as) said: "The oath is based on the intention."
records.arabicText: 4302 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ سَعْدٍ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْلِفُ بِالْيَمِينِ وَ ضَمِيرُهُ عَلَى غَيْرِ مَا حَلَفَ قَالَ "اَلْيَمِينُ عَلَى اَلضَّمِيرِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4302 - Isma'il ibn Sa'd asked Abu al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) about a man who swears an oath, but his intention in the oath differs from the apparent wording.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The oath is based on the intention."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4303 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a man who takes an oath and then forgets what he said.
Imam (as) replied: "It is based on what he intended."
records.arabicText: 4303 - وَ سَأَلَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ ، أَخَاهُ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْلِفُ وَ يَنْسَى مَا قَالَهُ قَالَ "هُوَ عَلَى مَا نَوَى".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4303 - Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a man who takes an oath and then forgets what he said.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is based on what he intended."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4304 - It is narrated from Sa'd ibn al-Hasan, from Abu Abdillah (as), that Imam (as) was asked about a man who swears that he will not sell his merchandise for a certain price, but then later changes his mind.
Imam (as) said: "He may sell it, and no expiation is required."
records.arabicText: 4304 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ اَلْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْلِفُ أَنْ لاَ يَبِيعَ سِلْعَتَهُ بِكَذَا وَ كَذَا ثُمَّ يَبْدُو لَهُ قَالَ "يَبِيعُ وَ لاَ يُكَفِّرُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4304 - It is narrated from Sa'd ibn al-Hasan, from Abu Abdillah (as), that Imam (as) was asked about a man who swears that he will not sell his merchandise for a certain price, but then later changes his mind.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He may sell it, and no expiation is required."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4305 - Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as)who said:
"If a man says, 'I swear' or 'I have taken an oath,' it is of no consequence until he says, 'I swear by Allah (swt)' or 'I have taken an oath by Allah (swt).'"
records.arabicText: 4305 - وَ رَوَى اَلسَّكُونِيُّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "إِذَا قَالَ اَلرَّجُلُ أَقْسَمْتُ أَوْ حَلَفْتُ فَلَيْسَ بِشَيْءٍ حَتَّى يَقُولَ أَقْسَمْتُ بِاللَّهِ أَوْ حَلَفْتُ بِاللَّهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4305 - Al-Sakuni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as)who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man says, 'I swear' or 'I have taken an oath,' it is of no consequence until he says, 'I swear by Allah (swt)' or 'I have taken an oath by Allah (swt).'"

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4306 - Aban narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding a man who said: "I am obligated to offer a camel," but did not specify where it should be slaughtered.
Imam (as) said: "The slaughtering is to be done at Mina, and it should be distributed among the needy."
records.arabicText: 4306 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَالَ عَلَيَّ بَدَنَةٌ وَ لَمْ يُسَمِّ أَيْنَ يَنْحَرُهَا قَالَ "إِنَّمَا اَلنَّحْرُ بِمِنًى يَقْسِمُهَا بَيْنَ اَلْمَسَاكِينِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4306 - Aban narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding a man who said: "I am obligated to offer a camel," but did not specify where it should be slaughtered.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The slaughtering is to be done at Mina, and it should be distributed among the needy."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.4307 - Muhammad ibn Yahya al-Khazzaz narrated from Talhah ibn Zayd, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) disliked that a man should feed others as expiation for an oath before breaking it.
records.arabicText: 4307 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى اَلْخَزَّازُ عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ زَيْدٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَرِهَ أَنْ يُطْعِمَ اَلرَّجُلُ فِي كَفَّارَةِ اَلْيَمِينِ قَبْلَ اَلْحِنْثِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4307 - Muhammad ibn Yahya al-Khazzaz narrated from Talhah ibn Zayd, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) disliked that a man should feed others as expiation for an oath before breaking it.

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4308 - Muhammad ibn Mansur asked Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a man who made a vow to fast but found fasting to be too difficult for him.
Imam (as) said: "He should give charity for each day in the amount of one mudd of wheat."
records.arabicText: 4308 - وَ سَأَلَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مَنْصُورٍ مُوسَى بْنَ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَذَرَ صِيَاماً فَثَقُلَ اَلصَّوْمُ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ "تَصَدَّقُ عَنْ كُلِّ يَوْمٍ بِمُدٍّ مِنْ حِنْطَةٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.4308 - Muhammad ibn Mansur asked Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) about a man who made a vow to fast but found fasting to be too difficult for him.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He should give charity for each day in the amount of one mudd of wheat."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4309 - Talhah ibn Zayd narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), regarding a pregnant woman who drank medicine and caused a miscarriage.
Imam (as) said: "She must offer expiation for it."
records.arabicText: 4309 - وَ رَوَى طَلْحَةُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اِمْرَأَةٍ حُبْلَى شَرِبَتْ دَوَاءً فَأَسْقَطَتْ قَالَ "تُكَفِّرُ عَنْهُ".

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4310 - The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, heard a man saying, "I am free from the religion of Muhammad."
The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, said to him: "Woe to you! If you are free from the religion of Muhammad, then whose religion will you follow?"
The Messenger of Allah (swt), peace be upon him and his family, never spoke to him again until he died.
records.arabicText: 4310 - وَ سَمِعَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ رَجُلاً يَقُولُ أَنَا بَرِيءٌ مِنْ دِينِ مُحَمَّدٍ فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "وَيْلَكَ إِذَا بَرِئْتَ مِنْ دِينِ مُحَمَّدٍ فَعَلَى دِينِ مَنْ تَكُونُ" فَمَا كَلَّمَهُ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ حَتَّى مَاتَ.

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4311 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il narrated from Salam ibn Sahm, the devout elder, that he heard Abu Abdillah (as) say to Sadir:
"O Sadir, whoever swears by Allah (swt) falsely has committed disbelief, and whoever swears by Allah (swt) truthfully has sinned. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, says:
'And do not make Allah (swt) an excuse in your oaths' (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:224)."
records.arabicText:
4311 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ عَنْ سَلاَّمِ بْنِ سَهْمٍ اَلشَّيْخِ اَلْمُتَعَبِّدِ أَنَّهُ سَمِعَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ لِسَدِيرٍ : "يَا سَدِيرُ إِنَّهُ مَنْ حَلَفَ بِاللَّهِ كَاذِباً كَفَرَ وَ مَنْ حَلَفَ بِاللَّهِ صَادِقاً أَثِمَ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَقُولُ:
۝ وَ لاٰ تَجْعَلُوا اَللّٰهَ عُرْضَةً لِأَيْمٰانِكُمْ ۝ "
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4311 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il narrated from Salam ibn Sahm, the devout elder, that he heard Abu Abdillah (as) say to Sadir:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"O Sadir, whoever swears by Allah (swt) falsely has committed disbelief, and whoever swears by Allah (swt) truthfully has sinned. Indeed, Allah (swt), the Almighty and Glorious, says:
'And do not make Allah (swt) an excuse in your oaths' (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:224)."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4312 - Abdullah ibn al-Qasim narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said that Abu Abdillah (as) said: "There is no valid oath taken in anger, nor in severing ties of kinship, nor under coercion, nor under compulsion."
I asked: "May Allah (swt) keep you well, what is the difference between coercion and compulsion?"
Imam (as) replied: "Compulsion is from a ruler, while coercion can come from a wife, a father, or a mother, and such an oath is of no consequence."
records.arabicText: 4312 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ اَلْقَاسِمِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ يَمِينَ فِي غَضَبٍ وَ لاَ فِي قَطِيعَةِ رَحِمٍ وَ لاَ فِي جَبْرٍ وَ لاَ فِي إِكْرَاهٍ" قَالَ قُلْتُ أَصْلَحَكَ اَللَّهُ فَمَا فَرْقٌ بَيْنَ اَلْإِكْرَاهِ وَ اَلْجَبْرِ قَالَ "اَلْجَبْرُ مِنَ اَلسُّلْطَانِ يَكُونُ وَ اَلْإِكْرَاهُ مِنَ اَلزَّوْجَةِ وَ اَلْأَبِ وَ اَلْأُمِّ وَ لَيْسَ ذَلِكَ بِشَيْءٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4312 - Abdullah ibn al-Qasim narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, who said that Abu Abdillah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no valid oath taken in anger, nor in severing ties of kinship, nor under coercion, nor under compulsion."
I asked: "May Allah (swt) keep you well, what is the difference between coercion and compulsion?"
Imam (as) replied: "Compulsion is from a ruler, while coercion can come from a wife, a father, or a mother, and such an oath is of no consequence."

records.chapter: Chapter on Oaths, Vows, and Expiations
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.4313 - And Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"Swear by Allah (swt) falsely and save your brother from being killed."
records.arabicText: 4313 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "اِحْلِفْ بِاللَّهِ كَاذِباً وَ أَنْجِ أَخَاكَ مِنَ اَلْقَتْلِ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.4313 - And Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Swear by Allah (swt) falsely and save your brother from being killed."
